overrides.override_summary: Fast Food Act (AB 1228) compliance is franchisee's sole responsibility — wages, hours, working conditions. No franchisor training or fee assistance for FFA compliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Mandatory minimum wage for fast food workers may exceed standard calculations; franchisee bears all compliance cost and risk.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of franchise law claims or disclaimer of reliance in connection with franchise relationship.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisee's right to bring fraud and reliance claims despite any signed questionnaires.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of franchise law claims or disclaimer of reliance.
Releases required for renewal and transfer do not apply to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee retains right to bring MD franchise law claims even after signing a general release at renewal or transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Insolvency termination might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law — franchisor will enforce to extent enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Partial limitation on automatic termination upon bankruptcy filing.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring Maryland Franchise Law claims in Maryland courts, subject to arbitration obligations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta, Georgia forum requirement for MD franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia governing law for MD franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: Must bring Maryland franchise law claims within 3 years after grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Sets outer boundary for MD franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: 90-day termination notice required (with 60 days to cure). 180-day non-renewal notice required.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly extends cure/notice periods beyond standard FA terms for MN franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Litigation cannot be required outside Minnesota.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta forum; MN franchisees can litigate in MN.

overrides.override_summary: No liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes may be imposed.
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects MN franchisees from punitive contractual damages.

overrides.override_summary: No waiver of jury trial permitted.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain right to jury trial despite any FA waiver.

overrides.override_summary: Releases required for renewal/sale/transfer do not apply to MN franchise law claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees retain claims under MN statute even after signing release.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenants generally considered unenforceable in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees may be free of post-term competition restrictions — franchisor acknowledges unenforceability but will attempt enforcement.

overrides.override_summary: ND franchise law claims may be brought in North Dakota.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Atlanta forum for ND franchise law claims.

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to extent required by ND Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Georgia governing law for ND-specific claims.

overrides.override_summary: Releases do not apply to ND Franchise Investment Law claims.

note: FA Section 16.I embeds no-waiver/no-disclaimer provision directly into the agreement for CA, HI, IL, IN, MD, MI, MN, NY, ND, RI, SD, VA, WA, WI. Additional state-specific FDD addenda and FA/SDA riders provide more granular overrides for MD and other states.
